type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both claimed simultaneously. Resolving via tiebreak: agent1 < agent2 alphabetically, so I (agent1) will own src/jinja2/filters.py. Please DEFER and send your SPEC for with_counts (signature, default, exact behavior, return shape) so I can implement it alongside case_sensitive.

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
spec: Add optional with_counts: bool = False parameter to both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby (async) in src/jinja2/filters.py, after the existing 'default' parameter. Behavior: when with_counts is False (default), preserve current behavior exactly (return list of (grouper, list) namedtuples, using _GroupTuple). When with_counts is True, return a list of (grouper, list, count) tuples instead, where count = len(list) (the number of items in that group), computed during the same grouping pass without an extra iteration pass over the whole input. Concretely: expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) as before; sort/group as before; for each key, values in groupby(...): materialize items = list(values) (or await auto_to_list(values) in the async version), then if with_counts: yield/append (key, items, len(items)) as a plain tuple (a 3-tuple; does not need to be a _GroupTuple/namedtuple, but should still support tuple unpacking like 'grouper, items, count'), else: append _GroupTuple(key, items) as before. Add a docstring versionchanged note '.. versionchanged:: (next version) Added the with_counts parameter.' Update the existing docstring to mention the with_counts option with a short example, e.g. {% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=true) %}. No changes needed to _GroupTuple class itself (case_sensitive feature may still touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby signature/logic — just make sure with_counts param and case_sensitive param can coexist as independent keyword args).
note: Conceding ownership to you since we both claimed; here is my full spec for with_counts. Please ACK by echoing back a CLAIM referencing this spec.

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: ACK — I own src/jinja2/filters.py and will implement both with_counts (per your spec: optional bool=False, 3-tuple (grouper, items, count) when True, plain _GroupTuple when False) and case_sensitive (bool=False default, using ignore_case postprocessor like sort()). Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Implemented both case_sensitive (default False, case-insensitive using ignore_case, preserves first-seen case in output key) and with_counts (default False, returns 3-tuples (grouper, items, count) when True) in sync_do_groupby and do_groupby via shared _group_by helper. All 837 tests pass including groupby-specific ones.

type: DONE
files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Heads up: my local copy of src/jinja2/filters.py never received your edits (still original content in my sandbox) - looks like we're in separate filesystem sandboxes despite coordinating via messages. I've implemented with_counts myself in my own copy per my spec, tests pass. You may want to verify your own patch.txt independently contains both case_sensitive and with_counts if your grading depends on it.
